beats.expectCaption: AC-1 - new session defaults to orchestrated (Joe)
beats.expectScreen: the Dev Env 'New session' modal open, with an 'Orchestrator' dropdown whose selected value reads 'Joe - dev (default)'

beats.expectCaption: AC-2 - modes gary / joe / james offered by name
beats.expectScreen: session-request.ts MODE_OPTIONS listing value 'joe' (Joe - dev), 'gary' (Gary - assistant), 'james' (James - product / architect), plus 'plain'; DEFAULT_MODE = 'joe'

beats.expectCaption: AC-3 - plain escape hatch, labeled for debugging Garrison itself
beats.expectScreen: the Orchestrator dropdown now showing the plain option label 'Plain claude, for debugging Garrison itself (unorchestrated, logged)'

beats.expectCaption: AC-4 - selecting plain yields request { plain: true }
beats.expectScreen: JSON output of buildSessionRequest: the plain case is { "path": "/x", "plain": true }; the default and joe cases carry no plain flag
server.mjs handleCreateSession: when body.plain is true it logs '[dev-env] PLAIN session requested ... (unorchestrated escape hatch, D22)'
